name: Housing Authority Of The City Of High Point
description_short: Public housing authority providing affordable rental housing, voucher programs, and free resident services including financial counseling, youth development, and homeownership education in High Point, NC.
description_long:
Housing Authority of the City of High Point (HPHA) is a government agency established to serve eligible families and individuals in High Point, North Carolina. Operating since at least the mid-20th century as a municipal housing authority, HPHA manages public housing communities and administers federal housing assistance programs at the local level. The organization operates under a mission to provide adequate and affordable housing, economic advancement, and homeownership opportunities in safe, drug-free environments without discrimination.

HPHA offers multiple housing and support programs. Their core offerings include Public Housing (directly managed rental properties), the Housing Choice Voucher Program (rental subsidies for private landlords), Family Self-Sufficiency counseling (employment, education, budgeting, credit, and homeownership guidance), Congregate Housing Services for elderly and disabled residents, and comprehensive Youth Services. The Youth Services program includes Project SOAR, which provides FAFSA completion assistance, financial literacy, college readiness support, and post-secondary program guidance to residents aged 15-20. Housing Counseling services support first-time homebuyers and those seeking homeownership education.

HPHA distinguishes itself as a government-operated, non-profit entity with no profit motive, making it uniquely positioned to prioritize resident welfare over financial returns. Their programs are free to eligible residents and funded through federal housing programs and local government appropriations. The organization emphasizes community development through real estate projects, HOPE VI revitalization initiatives, and infill development, demonstrating long-term commitment to neighborhood improvement beyond individual assistance.

As a public housing authority, HPHA is subject to government oversight, HUD regulations, and community accountability. Services are limited to eligible low-income families and individuals in the High Point area. Wait lists for public housing and vouchers may be lengthy, and eligibility requirements can be restrictive. The organization relies on government funding, which may affect program availability and responsiveness.
pros: Completely free financial counseling, housing counseling, and support services—no fees for eligible residents
Family Self-Sufficiency program offers comprehensive counseling in employment, education, budgeting, and credit-building
Youth-focused Project SOAR program provides free FAFSA completion, financial literacy, and college readiness support ages 15-20
Government-operated with no profit motive, ensuring resident interests are prioritized over financial gain
Congregate Housing Services provide supportive services for elderly and disabled residents to maintain independent living
Housing Choice Voucher Program provides rental subsidies, allowing residents choice in private rental markets
HUD-approved housing counseling and homeownership education for first-time buyers
cons: Services limited to High Point, NC geographic area and eligible low-income households only
Public housing and voucher programs likely have extended waitlists due to limited funding and high demand
Dependent on government funding and HUD regulations, which may limit program flexibility and responsiveness
Website provides limited details on application timelines, current waitlist length, or specific eligibility thresholds
As a government agency, processes may be slower than private sector alternatives
best_for: Low-income families and individuals seeking affordable rental housing in High Point, NC
Public housing residents seeking free financial counseling, credit building, and employment support
Youth ages 15-20 from public housing households needing college preparation and financial literacy education
Elderly and disabled residents requiring supportive services to maintain independent living
diagnosis: HPHA is best for low-income families and individuals in High Point, NC seeking free, government-backed affordable housing and financial counseling services. The primary caveat is that eligibility is restricted by income limits and geographic location, and publicly-funded housing programs typically involve lengthy waitlists and application processes.
services: Public Housing—directly managed affordable rental properties
Housing Choice Voucher Program—rental subsidies for use with private landlords
Family Self-Sufficiency counseling—employment, educational, homeownership, budgeting, and credit counseling
Youth Services—academic support, tutorials, summer camps, and athletic programs
Project SOAR—FAFSA completion, financial literacy, college readiness, and post-secondary assistance for ages 15-20
Congregate Housing Services Program—supportive services for elderly and disabled residents
Housing Counseling and Homeownership Education—guidance for first-time homebuyers
Real Estate and Development—property management and community revitalization projects
HOPE VI revitalization initiatives—neighborhood improvement and housing development
Infill Development projects—new affordable housing construction
Community partnerships and landlord support for voucher program participation
Donation and fundraising support through Seeds-to-Success Endowment Fund Scholarship
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 500 E Russell Ave, High Point, NC 27260
